Canonical says patched Ubuntu kernels are still pending for all releases and recommends disabling nested virtualization in the meantime.

【サイバーセキュリティ動向分析】  2026年7月12日時点の主なサイバーセキュリティニュースまとめ 以下に、最近（主に7月10〜11日頃に報じられた）注目すべきトピックを挙げ、各トピックの背景・影響・対策を長文で解説します。情報は公開報道に基づくもので、状況は流動的です。 1. npmパッケージ「jscrambler」のサプライチェーン攻撃（7月11日頃） 背景 jscramblerは、JavaScriptコードを難読化・保護する人気ツールで、npmレジストリを通じて配布され、週に約15,000人の開発者が利用しています。2026年7月11日頃、公式パッケージのバージョン8.14.0以降に悪意あるバージョンが混入されました。この悪意あるリリースには、インストール時にRust製のインフォスティーラー（情報窃取マルウェア）が自動的にドロップされる仕組みが仕込まれていました。マルウェアはクラウド認証情報、暗号通貨ウォレット、ブラウザ保存データ、AI APIキーなどを標的にし、Windows・macOS・Linuxのクロスプラットフォームで動作します。 Socket Securityなどのセキュリティツールが即座に検知し、jscrambler社が悪意あるバージョンを非推奨化。安全なバージョン8.22.0を迅速にリリースしました。現時点で悪意あるバージョンのダウンロードや実行報告は確認されていませんが、サプライチェーン攻撃の典型例です。 影響 開発者や企業が依存するツールにマルウェアが混入するリスクは極めて高く、クラウド環境やAI関連の機密情報が一括で盗まれる可能性があります。特にDevOpsやWeb開発チームで広く使われているため、連鎖的な情報漏洩やアカウント乗っ取りの連鎖が懸念されます。幸い被害は最小限に抑えられたようですが、こうした攻撃は「信頼できる公式パッケージ」への盲信を突くもので、ソフトウェアサプライチェーンの脆弱性を改めて浮き彫りにしました。中小企業や個人開発者への影響が特に大きい点も特徴です。 対策   直ちにpackage.jsonやlockfileを確認し、jscramblerのバージョンを8.22.0以上に更新する。 過去に悪意あるバージョンがインストールされた可能性がある場合は、関連するシークレット（APIキー、パスワードなど）をすべてローテーション。 npmパッケージのサプライチェーンセキュリティを強化するため、Socket Securityやnpm audit、SBOM（Software Bill of Materials）ツールの活用を推奨。 開発環境では「信頼できるソースからのみインストール」「署名検証の徹底」「最小権限の原則」を徹底。定期的な依存関係監査を習慣化することが重要です。 2. Progress ShareFileのStorage Zone Controllerに関する緊急セキュリティ脅威（7月10日） 背景 Progress Software（旧Citrix ShareFile）が提供するファイル共有・同期サービス「ShareFile」では、オンプレミスでファイルを管理するための「Storage Zone Controller」（Windowsサーバー上で動作）が提供されています。2026年7月10日、Progress社はこれらのコントローラーを運用する顧客に対し、サーバーの即時シャットダウンを指示しました。理由は「信頼できる外部セキュリティ脅威」の存在で、影響を受けたアカウントへのアクセスを一時的に無効化しています。 詳細な脅威の内容（攻撃者、具体的な脆弱性、侵入経路）は非公開で、現在社内外の専門家と調査中です。以前（2026年春頃）にStorage Zone Controllerのpre-auth RCE脆弱性（CVE-2026-2699など）が報告・修正されていましたが、今回の事案はそれとは別に「新たな信頼できる脅威」として扱われています。コントローラーはインターネットに露出しやすい位置に置かれるケースが多く、標的にされやすい構造です。 影響 オンプレミスでファイルを管理している企業・組織では、ファイル共有・同期サービスが完全に停止し、業務に大きな支障をきたします。特に機密文書や大容量ファイルを扱う業界（法律、医療、製造、金融など）への影響が深刻です。 現時点でアカウントやデータへの不正アクセスは確認されていませんが、サーバー自体が危険に晒されている可能性があり、ログの保全やインシデント対応が求められます。クラウドのみを利用している通常のShareFileユーザーへの直接影響はありません。 対策   Progress社の指示に従い、Storage Zone Controllerを即時シャットダウン。復旧は同社からの追加指示を待つ。 コントローラーのバージョンが5.12.4以降（5.x系）または6.x系であることを確認（過去の脆弱性対策済み版）。 インターネットに露出しているコントローラーがある場合は、インシデント対応プロセスを開始。ログを保全し、不審な.aspxファイルやストレージパス内の異常を調査。 代替としてクラウドのみのShareFile利用や、他社ファイル共有サービスの検討を。長期的にオンプレミスゲートウェイのセキュリティ監視（EDR導入、ネットワークセグメンテーション）を強化。 3. Zimbra CollaborationのClassic Web Clientにおけるstored XSS脆弱性（7月頃パッチ適用） 背景 Zimbra Collaboration Suite（ZCS）のClassic Web Clientに、stored cross-site scripting（XSS）脆弱性が存在しました。攻撃者が細工したメールを作成・送信すると、受信者がそのメールを開いた瞬間に悪意あるJavaScriptが実行され、セッション内で任意のコードが動作します。この脆弱性はメール本文に永続的に保存される「stored」タイプのため、メールを開くだけで攻撃が成立します。 Zimbra社は2026年7月頃にバージョン10.1.19で修正パッチをリリース。CVE識別子はまだ割り当てられていませんが、criticalレベルの深刻度とされています。過去にもZimbraのClassic UIでXSS脆弱性が複数報告されており、メールを攻撃ベクターとする事例が目立っています。 影響 メールを開くだけでメールボックス内の情報、セッションデータ、アカウント設定などにアクセスされる可能性があります。セッションハイジャックや追加のマルウェア感染、機密情報の窃取につながりやすく、組織のメールシステム全体が標的になるリスクがあります。特にClassic Web Clientを日常的に使用している企業・教育機関・公的機関への影響が大きいです。現時点で実環境での悪用報告はありませんが、メールは攻撃の入り口として非常に一般的であるため、潜在的な脅威は高いです。 対策   Zimbra Collaboration Suiteを直ちにバージョン10.1.19以上に更新。 Classic Web Clientを使用している場合は特に注意。可能であればModern Web Clientへの移行を検討。 メールフィルタリングの強化（不審なHTMLメールのブロック）、サンドボックス環境でのメール閲覧、ユーザー教育（不審メールの扱い方）。 Webアプリケーションの一般的な防御策（Content-Security-Policyの適切な設定、入力値の厳格なサニタイズ）を徹底。定期的な脆弱性スキャンを実施。 4. パキスタン・バローチスタン警察ポータルの悪用による中国・インド系スパイ活動（SentinelOneレポート、7月発表） 背景 2026年7月にSentinelOneが公開したレポートによると、2024年2月から2026年4月にかけて、中国系およびインド系とみられる複数の脅威アクターがパキスタンの法執行機関、特にバローチスタン警察を標的にしたスパイ活動を行っていました。 最も注目されたのは、バローチスタン警察の「Complaint Management System（苦情管理ポータル、.pk）」です。このポータルは警察職員だけでなく一般市民も利用する公開サービスで、攻撃者がここにマルウェアを「ポータルアップデート」として偽装してアップロード。訪問者（警察・市民）の端末に感染させる手法が用いられました。使用されたマルウェアにはPlugX、ShadowPad、Remcos、Cobalt Strikeなどが含まれ、中国系アクターはカスタムインプラントも展開しました。 影響 警察の内部データ（犯罪記録、生体情報、人事記録、市民苦情、ホテル・テナント登録情報など）が窃取されるリスクが極めて高く、地域の治安維持や国家安全保障に直結する情報が狙われました。バローチスタン地域は分離独立運動が活発で、地政学的に重要視されるエリアです。 中国系とインド系という「パートナーでもありライバルでもある」アクターが同じ標的に並行して活動した点が特徴的で、国家レベルのサイバー諜報活動の典型例です。影響はパキスタン国内の他の警察機関や、アジア・中東・欧州などに広がる広範な標的にも及びました。 対策   政府・法執行機関レベル：ネットワークのセグメンテーション、多要素認証の徹底、定期的なログ監査・脅威ハンティング、EDR/ XDRの導入。 ポータルやWebアプリケーションの更新時には、必ずデジタル署名検証とサンドボックスでのテストを実施。公式アップデート以外は絶対に実行しない。 一般企業・組織も参考に：サプライチェーンやサードパーティツールの検証を厳格化し、異常な更新プロセスに警戒。脅威インテリジェンスの共有（ISACなど）や国際的な情報交換を推進。 長期的に、国家支援型攻撃への防御として「ゼロトラストアーキテクチャ」の採用や、AIを活用した異常検知システムの強化が有効です。 これらのニュースは、サプライチェーン攻撃の増加、オンプレミスシステムの露出リスク、メールを介した攻撃の進化、国家支援型スパイ活動の活発化という現代のサイバー脅威の傾向を象徴しています。 組織は自社の環境を改めて見直し、迅速なパッチ適用、ログ監視、従業員教育を徹底してください。状況は日々変化する可能性があるため、信頼できるセキュリティベンダーや公式情報源からの最新情報を継続的に確認することをおすすめします。

Day 10 is posting a cyber attack everyday  Open an email. Lose your session.  Zimbra — the email/collaboration platform millions rely on — just shipped a patch for a nasty stored XSS bug in its Classic Web Client. No CVE yet, but here’s the kicker: you don’t even need to click anything. Just opening a crafted email is enough for an attacker to walk off with your session, your settings, or your entire mailbox. If you’re still on Classic UI, patch to ZCS 10.1.19. Don’t sit on this one.

Good security researchers come to the table with hard data right up front, can explain exploit vector(s) in question, include CVEs if applicable, explain risks and ramifications, and are forthcoming if they are unsure about something.  Bad security researchers come to the table with vague statements, can't provide even one exploit vector, can't explain risks, and won't share what data they do have whilst acting as if they're holding on to trade secrets.  Please be the former -- or if you aren't experienced enough to be the former yet, present your findings with humility (ex: "I ran this thing on your stuff, it reported these potential issues but I don't know if it's of importance. Here's the data").

Spare a thought for Hazel, who spent this morning howling at a machine that refused to exploit her.  The robot was down. An engineer had it in pieces by seven, some valve or other gone, and for three hours the one thing on the farm Hazel truly cares about was shut to her. She took it about as well as you'd expect from a cow who regards the daily milking limit as a slander on her good name.  You have to understand what the robot is to Hazel. The gate to her shed stands open all night, every night. Eighty cows lie in straw up to their hocks, free to stop where they are till Christmas, and most of them make a decent go of exactly that. Not Hazel. Hazel is in four times a day if the machine will have her, shouldering down the length of the shed in the pitch dark the moment her udder gets past comfortable, because full is an insult and Hazel answers insults promptly. A robot that wouldn't open at all was, to her, the collapse of a previously decent world.  She squared up to it and roared. She leaned on the gate that wouldn't read her collar, stood on the engineer's foot, took herself off in disgust, then came straight back to roar some more. By nine she had parked herself across the entrance so no other cow could reach the thing either, on the sound reasoning that if she wasn't being milked, the practice ought to be suspended nationally out of respect.  Roy watched the whole performance over a cup of tea he was in no hurry to finish.  "There's your forced," he said, to nobody in particular. "Can't keep her off the bloody thing."  They had it running by ten. Hazel was through the gate before the engineer had got to his feet, first in the queue, thoroughly milked and thoroughly vindicated, submitting once again to the terrible daily cruelty she will fight you tooth and hoof to be first in line for.

If you audit smart contracts, you already know @DeFiHackLabs. It's the most complete open library of exploit PoCs there is, and SunWeb3Sec has done the whole industry a service by maintaining it.  But a PoC is a starting point, not an explanation. To actually learn from one you have to: ➡ get the Foundry test compiling (solc version, EVM version, remappings)   ➡ source an archive RPC for the correct chain and fork at the exact block   ➡ reconstruct the attack from raw trace output   ➡ cross-reference the trace against the test's Solidity   ➡ pull the source of every downstream contract from Etherscan - the PoC ships the attacker's code, not the victim's.  We built crypto.training/hacks to collapse that into one page.  What you get per hack:  ✅ Pre-written vulnerability analysis, with the root-cause line highlighted directly in the source.   ✅ A live EVM playground (think in-browser debugger) that replays the exploit deterministically.   ✅ Opcode-level stepping plus line-level Next / Prev / Step-in / Step-out; click an opcode to jump to its source line.   ✅ Full call trace (execution tree) so you can see exactly what called what.   ✅ EVM state at every step: memory, stack, and storage.  ✅ Matched source for every executing contract in the call tree - fetched from Etherscan, compiled, and source-mapped, so you never leave the page.   ✅ Any chain, no RPC needed - the execution is recorded, so there's nothing to set up and nothing to rot: no rate limits, no stale fork blocks, no version drift.  The analysis + build pipeline is fully open source: github.com/sanbir/evm-hack-r… - issues and PRs welcome. Explore 840+ hacks 👉 crypto.training/hacks/ #SmartContractAudit #Web3Security #DeFi #Solidity #EVM #Foundry #Blockchain

An exchange kept taking your deposits while refusing to give withdrawals.  AscendEX quietly stopped its operations on July 1, but they made the announcement five days later, on July 6, and the reason on paper was missing EU licensing under MiCA.  Here's what they didn't lead with. ZachXBT had already flagged this exchange back on June 26. He checked their public hot wallets and found barely any $ETH, $USDT, or $SOL sitting there. Meanwhile deposits kept flowing in and withdrawals just stopped moving.  Now every withdrawal needs manual review. No timeline. No promise you get your full balance back. Just paperwork and silence.  ZachXBT says he's personally verified multiple users with 7 figure claims stuck on this platform, more than the wallets can even cover.  This isn't AscendEX's first time either. Lazarus Group drained $77 million from them in 2021. They said everyone would get made whole then too.  If your funds sat on a CEX you haven't checked in a while, check today. The wallets don't lie even when the exchange does.

The Commonwealth Ministerial Action Group (CMAG) held its seventy-third meeting in extraordinary session on Friday, 10 July 2026.  CMAG received with thanks the Report of the Secretary-General on the visit to the United Republic of Tanzania by Special Envoy, His Excellency Dr Lazarus McCarthy Chakwera, former President of the Republic of Malawi, together with an update on the Good Offices process.  In their concluding statement CMAG noted with concern that while one of the conditions has been met and another has been partially met, two other conditions remain unaddressed. CMAG also expressed concern that the situation in Tanzania has deteriorated since the Special Envoy’s visit, including a nationwide restriction on political activities, continuing pressure on civic space, on some social media platforms and media space generally, as well as the unresolved detention and legal proceedings of the principal opposition leader, and an atmosphere of fear among the opposition.   CMAG noted the recommendations of the Special Envoy for specific time-bound action as follows: a. Within 30 days: resolve the detention of Tundu Lissu through a political and legal pathway, and release others held solely for the peaceful exercise of rights. b. Within 60 days: restore full access to digital platforms and cease intimidation of media and free-expression advocates. c. Within 90 days: convene a credible, inclusive inter-party forum with neutral facilitation, with a mandate covering electoral-framework reform, political competition, detainees andaccountability. d. Initiate, without delay, the independent criminal investigation recommended by the Commission of Inquiry, including such issues as victim compensation, with defined eligibility, funding and timelines. #BREAKING: JUMUIYA YA MADOLA YAAMURU TANZANIA IMUACHIE LISSU NDANI YA SIKU 30 Jumuiya ya Commonwealth Ministerial Action Group (CMAG) imeipa Serikali ya Tanzania muda wa siku 30 kumwachia huru mwenyekiti a Chama Cha Demokrasia na Maendeleo ( CHADEMA) Tundu Lissu, kupitia njia ya kisiasa na kisheria.  Katika kikao chake cha 73 cha dharura, CMAG ilisisitiza kuwa “kuzuiliwa kwa Tundu Lissu lazima kutatuliwe, na wengine waliokamatwa kwa kutumia haki zao kwa amani waachiliwe.”  Kundi hilo limeonya kuwa kutotekeleza agizo hilo kutasababisha uchunguzi mkali zaidi wakati wa vikao vya Septemba na Novemba.  Mbali na kuachiliwa kwa Lissu, CMAG pia imeweka masharti mengine: kurejesha uhuru wa vyombo vya habari na majukwaa ya kidijitali ndani ya siku 60, na kuandaa jukwaa shirikishi la vyama vyote ndani ya siku 90.  Licha ya kutambua ushirikiano wa Tanzania na Mjumbe Maalum, Dkt. Lazarus Chakwera, CMAG Jumuiya hiyo imesema maendeleo ya mageuzi nchini humo yamekuwa madogo huku nafasi ya kiraia ikizidi kufungwa.

#BREAKING: COMMONWEALTH ORDERS TANZANIA TO FREE LISSU WITHIN 30 DAYS The Commonwealth Ministerial Action Group (CMAG) has issued a firm directive to Tanzania, demanding the release of opposition leader Tundu Lissu within 30 days.  Meeting in its 73rd extraordinary session on 10 July 2026, CMAG stressed "resolve the detention of Tundu Lissu through a Political and legal pathway , and release others held solely for peaceful exercise of rights."  The Group warned that failure to act would trigger further scrutiny when CMAG reconvenes in September and again in November.   Alongside Lissu’s release, CMAG set additional deadlines: restoring media and digital freedoms within 60 days, and convening an inclusive inter-party forum within 90 days.  While acknowledging Tanzania’s cooperation with the Special Envoy, Dr. Lazarus Chakwera, CMAG noted that progress on reforms remains limited and civic space continues to shrink.

🔴 Kim's Building His Mossad. Seoul's Building Excuses. 🔴  ​Kim Jong-un chaired the Party Central Military Commission plenary July 9 to expand and diversify the Reconnaissance General Bureau—North Korea's unified spy and cyber apparatus. This apparatus, headed by Gen. Ri Chang-ho and housing notorious cyber units like Lazarus Group and Kimsuky, is getting a massive structural upgrade. Pyongyang announced the shift, framing it as improving military intelligence capacity. Operations stayed classified.  ​Intelligence work is classified—both sides know it. But NK just signaled strategic intent: announce the structure, hide the tactics. That's how you show strength without exposing operational details. ​Seoul's response: silence. Not on the restructuring. Not on the threat. Not on policy. Just "we're closely monitoring"—the phrase for 'nothing changed.'  ​One side declared a military shift. The other side pretended it didn't happen. ​That's the asymmetry: not intelligence silence versus intelligence silence.   It's declared threat against strategic drift.   Can South Korean citizens truly say they are protected from these relentless, state-sponsored cyber threats?  ​#NorthKorea #Cybersecurity #Geopolitics

کرپٹو مارکیٹ کا المیہ: فومو (FOMO) کا اندھا لالچ، بے بس سرمایہ کار اور لاقانونیت کا عالمی سوالستان Part - 6 Zero Tolerance with Syed Haider Imam  "لوگ بھلے ہی مفتی صاحب کے فتوے کا لاکھ مذاق اڑائیں، لیکن ترقی پسندی اور لبرل ازم کی آڑ لے کر وہ ان تلخ حقائق سے نظریں نہیں چرا سکتے۔ مارکیٹ کے اس ہولناک سچ پر شتر مرغ کی طرح ریت میں سر چھپا لینے سے حقیقت بدل نہیں جائے گی۔"  پاکستان میں کرپٹو فراڈز کے ہولناک وار اور معاشی نقصان  عالمی سطح کے ان جھٹکوں کے ساتھ ساتھ پاکستان کی مقامی ڈیجیٹل مارکیٹ بھی پونزی اسکیموں اور فراڈ کا شکار ہو کر اربوں روپے گنوا چکی ہے، جہاں ریگولیشن نہ ہونے کا فائدہ اٹھا کر ہیکرز نے بائننس (Binance) سے منسلک 11 جعلی موبائل ایپس (جیسے MCX، HFC اور HTFOX) کے ذریعے 2022 میں 37,000 سے زائد پاکستانیوں کی عمر بھر کی جمع پونجی یعنی 100 ملین ڈالر (تقریباً 17.7 ارب روپے) لوٹ لی۔ اس کے علاوہ، دسمبر 2025 میں نیشنل سائبر کرائم انویسٹی گیشن ایجنسی (NCCIA) نے ایک اور بین الاقوامی کرپٹو فوریکس نیٹ ورک کا خاتمہ کر کے 60 ملین ڈالر (تقریباً 16.5 ارب روپے) کا فراڈ پکڑا اور 34 ملزمان کو گرفتار کیا، جبکہ فنانس جرائد کے مطابق "ٹریژر این ایف ٹی" (Treasure NFT) نامی لالچ پر مبنی اسکیم میں پاکستانیوں نے آنکھیں بند کر کے سرمایہ کاری کی جس سے مجموعی طور پر 143.8 ملین ڈالر (تقریباً 44.40 ارب روپے) کا ہولناک نقصان ہوا۔ اس سے نہ صرف سرمایہ کار ذاتی طور پر برباد ہوئے بلکہ اسٹیٹ بینک آف پاکستان (SBP) اور ایکسچینج کمپنیز ایسوسی ایشن کی حالیہ رپورٹ کے مطابق ایک سال کے اندر پاکستان سے 600 ملین ڈالر (تقریباً 166 ارب روپے) غیر قانونی طور پر کرپٹو مارکیٹوں میں منتقل ہوئے، جس نے ملکی زرمبادلہ کے ذخائر پر شدید دباؤ ڈالا۔ NCCIA کے مطابق یہ تمام فراڈز یوٹیوب، واٹس ایپ اور فیس بک پر "راتوں رات امیر ہونے" کا جھوٹا جھانسا دے کر کیے جاتے ہیں جہاں اوسطاً ہر متاثرہ شخص ہزاروں سے لاکھوں روپے گنوا بیٹھتا ہے۔  ہیکنگ کا تضاد اور اربوں ڈالرز کی چوریاں  اس بیرونی حکومتی کنٹرول کے ساتھ ساتھ، سیکیورٹی کے بڑے بڑے دعووں کے باوجود ہیکرز کا نقب لگانا ایک اور بڑا تضاد بن کر ابھرا ہے، جہاں اور نفع کے لالچ میں لاکھوں لوگ ہیکرز اور مارکیٹ کریش کے ہاتھوں اپنا لائف ٹائم سرمایہ ڈبو بیٹھے ہیں۔ صرف ایم ٹی گاکس (Mt. Gox) اور بٹ فائنیکس (Bitfinex) جیسے چند تاریخی ہیکس میں ہی مجموعی طور پر 9 لاکھ سے زائد اصل بٹ کوائن (900,000+ BTC) چوری ہو گئے۔ بلاک چین تجزیاتی کمپنی Chainalysis کے ریکارڈ کے مطابق پچھلے 4 سالوں (2022 سے 2025 تک) میں سائبر مجرم مجموعی طور پر 12 ارب ڈالر سے زائد مالیت کے کرپٹو اثاثے اڑا چکے ہیں، جس میں ہیکنگ کے بے تاج بادشاہ یعنی شمالی کوریا کے سرکاری "لازارس گروپ" (Lazarus Group) نے اکیلے اب تک 6.75 ارب ڈالر سے زائد کی کرپٹو چوری کا ریکارڈ قائم کر کے اس ڈیجیٹل تجوری کی قلعی کھول دی ہے۔  کرپٹو مارکیٹ کے میگا فراڈز اور سرمایہ کاروں کا صفایا  کرپٹو سیکٹر کا ایک اور تاریک ترین تضاد اس میں ہونے والے تاریخ کے بڑے ترین مالیاتی فراڈز (Financial Scams) ہیں، جہاں سیکیورٹی اور شفافیت کے نام پر معصوم سرمایہ کاروں سے اربوں ڈالرز لوٹ لیے گئے۔ اس انڈسٹری کا سب سے بدنام زمانہ اسکینڈل FTX ایکسچینج کا کریش تھا، جس کے بانی سیم بینک مین فریڈ (Sam Bankman-Fried) نے صارفین کے 8 ارب ڈالر سے زائد کے فنڈز کا خفیہ غبن کیا اور مارکیٹ کو مٹی میں ملا دیا۔ اسی طرح دو کوون (Do Kwon) کا تخلیق کردہ Luna اور TerraUSD نیٹ ورک محض چند دنوں میں زیرو ہو گیا، جس سے دنیا بھر کے سرمایہ کاروں کے 40 ارب ڈالر پلک جھپکتے ہی ڈوب گئے۔ مزید برآں، روجا اگناٹووا (Ruja Ignatova)، جسے دنیا "کرپٹو کوئین" (Cryptoqueen) کے نام سے جانتی ہے، نے OneCoin نامی پونزی اسکیم (Ponzi Scheme) کے ذریعے دنیا بھر سے 4 ارب ڈالر کا فراڈ کیا اور آج تک ایف بی آئی (FBI) کی انتہائی مطلوب مفرور ملزمان کی لسٹ میں شامل ہے۔ نفع کے لالچ اور کسی حکومتی ریگولیشن کے نہ ہونے کی وجہ سے ان میگا فراڈز نے لاکھوں خاندانوں کو سڑک پر لا کھڑا کیا، جس نے اس ڈیجیٹل مارکیٹ کی ساکھ کو ناقابل تلافی نقصان پہنچایا ہے۔

CRYPTO WEEKLY UPDATE — SUNDAY, 12 JULY 2026  Crypto spent this week caught between two very different realities.  On the surface, Bitcoin recovered towards $64,000 and several indicators suggested that the worst selling pressure may be fading.  Underneath, however, institutional demand remained inconsistent, Strategy sold Bitcoin to meet financial obligations, and renewed fighting around the Strait of Hormuz threatened to push oil, inflation and interest-rate expectations higher.  At the same time, some of the most important developments had little to do with prices.  Banks began testing blockchain-based cross-border payments.   Circle secured a US national trust-bank charter.   Stablecoins moved through a real Hyundai corporate payment.   Europe tightened its regulatory framework.   And Ethereum published a longer-term plan focussed on scalability, privacy and protection from quantum computers.  The story of the week was therefore not “crypto is bullish again”.  It was this:  The market may be attempting to form a bottom while the financial infrastructure around crypto continues to mature.  🟢 MARKETS  Bitcoin was trading near $63,900 at the time of writing, after recovering from its late-June low near $58,000.  That resilience matters—but it is not yet proof of a new bull market.  The rebound has occurred with relatively restrained leverage and only intermittent ETF demand.  US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded approximately $90.4 million of net inflows on July 10, but that followed two negative sessions.  Bitcoin’s more important test arrives on Tuesday, 14 July, when the United States publishes June inflation data.  Why it matters:  Higher-than-expected inflation could push government-bond yields and the US dollar higher and increase expectations that interest rates will remain elevated—or rise further.  That normally makes speculative investments less attractive.  A softer inflation report could support Bitcoin’s rebound.  A hotter report, particularly alongside rising oil prices, could put the $60,000 area under pressure again.  🟢 THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ BECAME CRYPTO’S BIGGEST MACRO RISK  The US–Iran conflict intensified sharply over the weekend.  US forces said they struck approximately 140 Iranian military targets on Saturday after Iran attacked a Cyprus-flagged commercial ship. Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and launched further attacks or attempted attacks across the region.  The Strait previously carried roughly one-fifth of global oil supplies, making it one of the world’s most important energy routes.  This is not primarily a crypto story—but it may become the most important crypto catalyst.  A prolonged disruption could:  • raise oil and transport costs;  • increase inflation;  • reduce the probability of lower interest rates;  • strengthen demand for cash and the US dollar;  • weaken appetite for risk assets.  Bitcoin is sometimes described as “digital gold”, but during sudden geopolitical shocks it has often behaved more like a liquidity-sensitive technology asset.  🟢 BITCOIN: BOTTOMING SIGNALS ARE APPEARING, BUT CONFIRMATION IS MISSING  Several long-term indicators now suggest that Bitcoin is becoming historically cheap.  Its June monthly relative strength index reportedly fell towards levels last seen around the 2022 bear-market bottom.  Simple explanation:  The relative strength index, or RSI, measures the speed and strength of recent price moves.  A low reading suggests that selling has been unusually intense. It does not tell us exactly when selling will stop.  Bitcoin has also reached an extremely depressed valuation relative to gold.  One BTC-versus-gold oscillator fell approximately 1.81 standard deviations below its long-term trend—the lowest reported reading since 2010.  That may indicate that Bitcoin is unusually cheap compared with another scarce asset.  The more convincing bottoming argument comes from the combination of several factors:  • Bitcoin has already fallen by roughly half from its October 2025 high.  • A large proportion of holders are sitting on unrealised losses.  • Long-term momentum remains bearish, but the rate of deterioration appears to be slowing.  • Negative sentiment and short positioning have increased.  • Long-term holders appear less willing to sell than during the previous phase of the cycle.  Translation:  The market may be moving from aggressive selling into exhaustion.  However, exhaustion is not the same as renewed demand.  Bitcoin’s central problem in 2026 has not simply been that everybody wants to sell. It has been that too few committed buyers have been willing to absorb the available supply.  Until ETF flows, spot volumes and institutional accumulation improve consistently, any rally should be treated as a possible relief rally rather than a confirmed new uptrend.  🟢 STRATEGY SOLD BITCOIN—AND THAT CHANGED THE TREASURY NARRATIVE  Strategy sold 3,588 BTC for approximately $216 million between June 29 and July 5.  It used the proceeds to pay preferred-share distributions and replenish its dollar reserve.  Following the sale, Strategy held:  • 843,775 BTC; • approximately $2.55 billion in dollar reserves; • an average Bitcoin acquisition price of $75,476.  This was a small disposal relative to its total holdings, so it does not prove that Strategy has abandoned its long-term Bitcoin thesis.  But it does reveal something important.  Strategy is no longer only a buyer.  Its financing structure now includes substantial dividend and interest obligations. When its shares or preferred securities trade poorly, raising fresh capital becomes more difficult and selling a portion of its Bitcoin becomes one available balance-sheet tool.  Simple explanation:  Strategy has used shares and debt-like securities to raise money and buy Bitcoin.  That works extremely well when investors are eager to fund the company. It becomes more complicated when Bitcoin falls and those securities lose value, because the company still needs cash to meet its obligations.  This creates two risks. First, Strategy may be a less reliable source of constant Bitcoin demand than it was during the bull market. Second, holding more than 4% of Bitcoin’s eventual 21-million supply creates concentration risk.  A forced sale is not the base case today.  Strategy has significant reserves and described the latest sale as deliberate financial management.  But the market must now analyse both sides of its balance sheet: its enormous Bitcoin holdings and the obligations built around them.  The broader lesson is simple:  A company holding Bitcoin is not the same investment as Bitcoin itself.  Shareholders also inherit corporate debt, dividend commitments, dilution risk, management decisions and financing conditions.  🟢  INSTITUTIONS AND REGULATED INFRASTRUCTURE  The week’s most significant structural development came from Swift.  Swift launched a blockchain-based shared-ledger pilot involving 17 banks across six continents, including major institutions such as Citi, HSBC, UBS, BNP Paribas, Wells Fargo and Standard Chartered.  The system will allow participating banks to move tokenised deposits around the clock before completing final settlement through existing financial infrastructure.  Simple explanation:  A tokenised deposit is similar to a stablecoin, but it is not the same thing.  A stablecoin such as USDC or USDT is a digital token issued by a private company and designed to remain worth approximately one dollar.  A tokenised deposit represents money that is already held in a commercial bank account. It is still ordinary bank money, but it is given a digital blockchain-style form so that it can move faster and operate with programmable rules.  Think of it this way:  A stablecoin is like exchanging your bank money for a digital token issued by another company.  A tokenised deposit is your existing bank money placed inside a digital wrapper.  The key difference is who owes you the money.  With a stablecoin, the issuer—such as Circle or Tether—owes you the value.  With a tokenised deposit, the commercial bank remains responsible for the money.  Tokenised deposits are therefore more likely to operate within regulated banking networks, while stablecoins can circulate more widely across public blockchains.  Why it matters:  Swift connects more than 11,500 financial organisations. It is not trying to replace the existing banking system.  Instead, it is testing whether blockchain technology can make bank money move faster, operate around the clock and remain fully compatible with today’s compliance, settlement and risk-management systems.  This shows that major banks are embracing blockchain to modernise traditional banking rather than replace it.  🟢 STABLECOINS MOVED FURTHER INTO THE REAL ECONOMY  Circle received final approval from the US Office of the Comptroller of the Currency to establish Circle National Trust.  The trust bank will provide digital-asset custody and strengthen the regulated infrastructure surrounding USDC.  Simple explanation:  USDC is a stablecoin—a digital token designed to remain worth approximately one US dollar.  Although Circle is becoming a national trust bank, this is not the same as becoming an ordinary retail bank. Circle will not suddenly begin offering everyday current accounts or mortgages.  Instead, the new charter places Circle under federal supervision, making institutions potentially more comfortable using its infrastructure for digital-asset custody, settlement and blockchain-based financial products.  That could prove far more important over the long term than short-term movements in Circle’s share price.  Hyundai Card also completed a real corporate stablecoin payment between Hyundai Motor’s US and Mexican operations.  The company converted $20,000 into USDT, transferred it over the Avalanche blockchain, and converted it back into dollars in Mexico. The process reportedly took about seven minutes, compared with several hours through traditional interbank payment systems.  The amount was small, and this was a proof of concept rather than mass adoption.  But it demonstrated a genuine commercial use of stablecoins rather than another theoretical blockchain experiment.  Hyundai’s next trial is expected to involve European entities, Visa and Circle.  Why it matters:  These developments show that stablecoins are beginning to solve real business problems rather than simply serving crypto traders.  For companies, the attraction is straightforward: faster settlement, lower administrative costs and payments that can operate around the clock.  Taken together with Swift’s tokenised-deposit pilot, they point to the same long-term trend.  Banks are modernising how bank money moves.  Companies are beginning to use stablecoins for real commercial payments.  Blockchain may therefore become part of everyday financial infrastructure long before most consumers even realise they are using it.  🟢 REGULATION  🔶The US CLARITY Act remains one of the industry’s most important potential catalysts.  The legislation would define more clearly how responsibility for digital assets is divided between the Securities and Exchange Commission and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission.  The Senate Banking Committee advanced the bill by 15 votes to nine in May, but it still needs enough Senate support, floor time and agreement on disputed provisions.  Key disagreements include:  • rules protecting software developers who do not control customer funds;  • anti-money-laundering enforcement;  • stablecoin rewards;  • ethical restrictions on elected officials profiting from crypto projects.  Simple explanation:  US crypto regulation currently resembles a football match in which two referees sometimes disagree about the rules and even about which one is in charge.  The CLARITY Act aims to define the pitch, the rules and the responsibilities before play begins.  Why it matters:  Clear rules could encourage exchanges, asset managers and banks to invest more confidently.  Poorly designed rules, however, could weaken consumer protection or create loopholes.  The market should therefore distinguish between legislation being discussed, legislation passing a committee and legislation actually becoming law.  No final passage is guaranteed.  🔶 Europe also entered the post-transition phase of its Markets in Crypto-Assets regime on July 1.  🔶 Ripple received full authorisation from Luxembourg’s regulator to offer regulated crypto-payment services across the 30 countries of the European Economic Area.  🔶 Meanwhile, the European Securities and Markets Authority began a review of how authorised crypto companies manage custody, private keys, transaction controls, cyber incidents, smart contracts and third-party risks.  This is a healthy evolution. Licensing is only the first step.  Regulators must also test whether licensed companies can protect customers during real operational failures.  🟢 ETHEREUM  Ethereum’s most important development was not a price move.  Vitalik Buterin outlined a multi-year “Lean Ethereum” direction centred on:  • scalability; • faster settlement; • improved privacy; • cheaper verification; • more efficient data storage; • resistance to future quantum computers.  Simple explanation:  Ethereum is trying to rebuild important parts of its engine while the vehicle is still moving.  That is ambitious and necessary—but technically difficult.  Quantum resistance matters because sufficiently powerful future quantum computers could eventually threaten the cryptography used to protect blockchain accounts.  This is not an immediate crisis.  No known quantum computer can currently break Ethereum or Bitcoin’s security at scale.  However, blockchains intended to secure assets for decades must begin preparing years before the threat becomes practical.  Ethereum’s advantage is that it has a detailed research community and an established upgrade process.  Its disadvantage is coordination.  A three-to-four-year roadmap is not a delivered product. Banks and asset managers will judge Ethereum by what is successfully implemented, not by the elegance of its research proposals.  Ethereum therefore has a stronger long-term technical vision—but also a clearer execution test.  🟢 DEFI IS BECOMING INVISIBLE INFRASTRUCTURE  Aave Labs launched Stable Vaults, enabling fintech companies, wallets, exchanges and payment apps to offer stablecoin earnings without building their own complicated DeFi systems.  Simple explanation: Customers may see what looks like a straightforward digital savings product. Behind the scenes, Aave allocates the deposited stablecoins across approved blockchain lending markets and vaults. This represents an important transition. During DeFi’s first phase, users visited individual protocols, moved assets between blockchains and managed technical risks themselves. In the next phase, DeFi may operate quietly behind familiar financial apps.  That can expand adoption, but it does not remove risk.  The yield still comes from underlying lending and investment activity. Smart-contract failures, liquidity shortages, bridge problems and stablecoin de-pegging remain possible even when the customer interface looks simple.  A polished product is not the same as a guaranteed deposit account.  🟢 SECURITY  The reported BonkDAO treasury attack, in which a malicious governance proposal allegedly drained approximately $20 million in BONK, was an important reminder of governance risk.  Blockchain systems can function exactly as coded and still produce disastrous outcomes if attackers gain control of voting or proposal mechanisms.  Simple explanation:  A smart contract can be compared with an automatic vault.  The vault may work perfectly, but if criminals obtain the authorised keys or manipulate the voting process, the vault will faithfully follow the criminals’ instructions.  Investors should therefore assess:  • who can submit proposals; • who can vote; • how quickly proposals can execute; • whether large transfers have time delays; • whether emergency controls exist.  “Decentralised” does not automatically mean “secure”.  🟢 WHAT INVESTORS SHOULD WATCH NEXT  The next stage of the market will probably be decided by five variables:  Tuesday’s US inflation report.  Oil prices and whether traffic through the Strait of Hormuz normalises.  Whether Bitcoin ETF inflows become consistent rather than appearing for isolated sessions.  Whether Strategy’s latest Bitcoin sale remains exceptional or becomes recurring.  Whether Bitcoin can hold the $60,000 region while spot demand and trading volume recover.  The evidence is gradually becoming less bearish.  But “less bearish” is not the same as bullish.  Technical indicators suggest the market is historically stretched. Long-term adoption continues. Stablecoins and tokenised bank money are moving into real financial operations.  Yet price remains governed by liquidity and demand.  That is the tension defining crypto today:  The infrastructure may be advancing faster than the market.  Patient investors should respect both sides of that reality.  Sources:  US SEC filings; US Bureau of Labor Statistics; Federal Reserve; OCC and Circle; Senate Banking Committee; Reuters; AP; Swift; Ripple; ESMA; Ethereum.org; CoinDesk; Crypto fund-flow data.  ✨ Your weekly snapshot of the crypto world in one post.  #Bitcoin #Crypto #Ethereum

Cloudflare's Cloudforce One team dropped their 2026 Threat Intelligence Report last week, and the headline finding is essentially this: state-sponsored cyber operations have graduated from targeted intrusions to something closer to industrial production lines running continuously against democratic and critical infrastructure targets. The data behind it comes from trillions of network signals, which is not a marketing number. Cloudflare sits in the path of a genuinely massive slice of global internet traffic, so their visibility into attack patterns is qualitatively different from what you get out of incident response engagements or government disclosure cycles.  Here's where I want to focus, because the report itself will get covered plenty elsewhere.  The living-off-the-land piece is the thing that should be driving planning conversations right now. Tier 1 state groups, Russia's SVR and GRU units, China's APT40 and related clusters, have standardized on using legitimate commercial infrastructure as their operational cover. Cloudflare's own services. AWS. Azure. Legitimate VPN exits. The effect is that a defender watching for "suspicious" traffic increasingly cannot distinguish it from normal business activity without very deep behavioral analysis. The detection problem is getting harder faster than most enterprise security programs are adapting. And the report's disclosure of which specific TTPs have been caught is genuinely double-edged: it helps defenders prioritize, but the groups reading that same report will rotate off flagged behaviors within a cycle or two.  Now layer in the calendar. The 2026 midterms are the targeting backdrop for everything happening between now and November. Russia, China, and Iran each have documented, sustained interest in U.S. electoral processes, and they do not have identical objectives. Russia's operational interest skews toward amplifying polarization and undermining confidence in results, the 2016 and 2020 playbooks both. China's interest is more granular: specific candidates, specific policy positions on Taiwan and trade, specific legislative staffers who touch those issues. Iran's operations have been more retaliatory and reactive, see the 2024 hack-and-leak operation against the Trump campaign, but that doesn't make them less disruptive. Three actors, three different threat models, all converging on the same six-month window.  What Cloudflare's network position actually captures before anyone else does is the reconnaissance and pre-positioning phase. The DNS lookups against legislative networks. The scanning of political party infrastructure. The probing of county election system vendors. That activity happens well before any exploitation, and it's the phase where defenders theoretically have the most room to act. The problem is that most of the organizations being probed, state party committees, county election offices, small campaigns, do not have the security teams to act on that kind of signal even if someone handed it to them. The intelligence-to-action pipeline breaks down at the last mile almost every time.  So what should people actually be watching for and doing with this.  For officials and campaign security teams: the pre-positioning phase for November operations is probably already underway or imminent. The midterm targeting cycle historically starts heating up 6 to 8 months out. If your organization handles anything touching election administration, candidate communications, or legislative work on China or Russia policy, and you do not have MFA enforced universally, you are an easy target. Not a sophisticated target. An easy one. The SVR does not need to burn a zero-day on an Exchange server with password spraying still working fine.  For people watching the information environment side: the cyber intrusions are frequently the predicate for the influence operation, not a separate track. The hack-and-leak model, steal authentic material and release it through cutouts or laundered through domestic amplifiers, is the playbook. The 2024 Iran operation targeted the Trump campaign specifically to create that material. The GRU's 2016 operation against the DNC and Podesta was the same structure. So when you see a cyber intrusion attributed to a state actor against a political target, the relevant downstream question is not just "what did they access" but "when does this show up on Telegram or in a friendly outlet."  For defenders doing the technical work: the living-off-the-land normalization has a practical implication for your detection architecture. Signature-based and known-bad-indicator detection is increasingly insufficient against the top-tier groups. Behavioral baselines, what does normal look like for this network, this user, this service account, are what create detection opportunities against actors who are deliberately mimicking legitimate traffic. That's resource-intensive to build and maintain, but reports like this one make the case for why the investment is justified.  One more thing on the structural dynamic the report describes. Public threat intelligence disclosures accelerate adversary adaptation. Every time a TTP gets named and published, the groups running it have a roadmap for what to retire. The Cloudforce One team knows this, every serious threat intelligence team does. They publish anyway because the defensive value of broad awareness outweighs the cost of tipping off actors who are already highly capable. But it means the gap between "detected TTP" and "evolved TTP" is measured in weeks to a few months now, not years. The 2026 report's findings are accurate as of the data collection window. Some of those techniques are already being rotated out by the groups that read it.  The trajectory here is not toward more dramatic, visible attacks. It's toward more persistent, lower-visibility pre-positioning, the kind that doesn't get noticed until after an election cycle ends or a policy decision lands and someone goes back to look at the logs. That's a harder problem to defend against politically and practically than a dramatic infrastructure attack would be, and the groups running these programs understand that.  foreigninterference.org/post… #foreigninterference #AdvancedPersistentThreatOperations #LivingOffTheLandExploitation #ThreatIntelligenceBulletin #CriticalInfrastructureMapping #VulnerabilityScanning

⚠️ रोकारोला (Rocarola) मालवेयर से सावधान  रोकारोला (Rocarola) एक नया और अत्यंत खतरनाक Android Banking Trojan है, जो 200 से अधिक बैंकिंग, वित्तीय एवं क्रिप्टोकरेंसी ऐप्स को निशाना बना रहा है। यह संक्रमित स्मार्टफोन पर उन्नत रिमोट कमांड के माध्यम से लगभग पूरा नियंत्रण हासिल कर सकता है।  यह मालवेयर क्या कर सकता है?  🔹 डिवाइस का लॉक-स्क्रीन PIN चुरा सकता है। 🔹 SMS पढ़ और भेज सकता है, जिससे OTP चोरी की जा सकती है। 🔹 डिवाइस पर इंस्टॉल ऐप्स को दूर से नियंत्रित कर सकता है। 🔹 लाइव स्क्रीन एक्टिविटी रिकॉर्ड कर सकता है। 🔹 Google Play Protect को निष्क्रिय (Disable) करने की कोशिश कर सकता है।  वित्तीय खतरा (Financial Impact)  यह मालवेयर Overlay Attack के माध्यम से बैंकिंग और डिजिटल वॉलेट ऐप्स की असली लॉगिन स्क्रीन पर नकली स्क्रीन दिखाकर यूज़रनेम, पासवर्ड और अन्य संवेदनशील जानकारी चुरा लेता है।  इसके अलावा, यह Clipboard Hijacking के जरिए कॉपी किए गए क्रिप्टोकरेंसी वॉलेट एड्रेस को बदल सकता है, जिससे आपकी राशि किसी हमलावर के वॉलेट में ट्रांसफर हो सकती है।  संक्रमण कैसे फैलता है? (Infection Method)  🔸 Chrome, TikTok या अन्य लोकप्रिय ऐप्स के नकली संस्करणों के माध्यम से। 🔸 दुर्भावनापूर्ण (Malicious) वेबसाइटों से APK डाउनलोड कराने के जरिए। 🔸 शुरुआती डाउनलोड एक Dropper होता है, जो Google Play Protect जैसा दिखकर यूज़र का भरोसा जीतता है और आवश्यक अनुमतियाँ (Permissions) प्राप्त कर लेता है।  हमले की तकनीक (Attack Technique)  यह मालवेयर Accessibility Service का दुरुपयोग कर पूरे डिवाइस पर नियंत्रण स्थापित करता है।  इसके बाद यह:  - डायनेमिक HTML फ़िशिंग ओवरले लोड करता है। - C2 (Command & Control) सर्वर से कमांड प्राप्त करता है। - 100 से अधिक प्रकार के रिमोट निर्देशों को निष्पादित करने में सक्षम है।  सुरक्षित कैसे रहें?  ✅ केवल आधिकारिक App Store (Google Play Store) से ही ऐप इंस्टॉल करें। ✅ अनावश्यक Accessibility Permissions कभी न दें। ✅ अज्ञात स्रोतों से APK फ़ाइल डाउनलोड करने से बचें। ✅ Google Play Protect को हमेशा सक्रिय (Enabled) रखें। ✅ यदि किसी ऐप का व्यवहार असामान्य लगे, तो उसे तुरंत हटाएँ और डिवाइस की सुरक्षा जाँच करें।  ⚠️ सतर्क रहें, सुरक्षित रहें। साइबर सुरक्षा आपकी पहली जिम्मेदारी है।  — Cyber Expert Ritesh Kumar #cybershieldwithtara #security #cybedost @Cyberdost @IPRDBihar @bihar_police

forced deprecation of mv2 extensions is only part of the problem. the chrome web store is generally hostile to everything that doesn't benefit google, and i don't think it should be the only place for all chromium browsers to get extensions.  google likes to block genuine extensions from releasing updates (or from being published at all), yet it rarely takes down active malware in a timely manner, if at all. it's surprising an alternative web store hasn't been created yet, but not unexpected, considering browser vendors rarely do anything for everyone's benefit without a hidden incentive. see "microsoft edge add-ons" or "opera extensions", for example. they're locked to a vendor's "ecosystem" for no real reason.  the biggest blocker here is safety and moderation, because every extension would have to be thoroughly scanned for malicious behavior, and i don't think existing software is good enough for that. it would likely be the biggest expense for this project.  it'd be great if we had someone dedicated to this on the helium team, but since we're *really* tight on budget, we'd have to work even harder and stretch ourselves even thinner for the benefit of an open web.  i'm not sure if we'll be able to do this, so don't take this as a promise or announcement or anything serious, just thinking out loud lol

2/3: The bug is in how HTTP/3 compresses headers via QPACK. Any server embedding XQUIC with default QPACK settings is exposed, including Tengine, which fronts Alibaba sites like Taobao and Alipay. Every release through v1.9.4 is affected. #CyberSecurity #ZeroDay #InfoSec

1/3: A flaw nicknamed XRING in XQUIC, Alibaba HTTP/3 library, lets any remote client crash the server with about 260 bytes of legal QUIC traffic. No login needed, no malformed packets, and there is no patch yet. #CyberSecurity #ZeroDay #InfoSec #TechNews

sample_tweets.text: Every crypto platform rewards you for depositing.  Almost none reward you for coming back.  ---  That is the real retention problem.  Users sign up. They check in once. They disappear.  Not because the product failed them.  Because there was nothing there tomorrow.  No reason to open the app. No habit loop. No stakes.  ---  BALIA OS is building the Daily Chest system, coming with Phase 0.  A reward mechanic being designed around one behavior: showing up every day.  Here is how it works:  → You open your chest once per day (UTC reset — no loophole) → Your streak counter tracks consecutive daily opens → The longer the streak, the better the chest tier: Bronze → Silver → Gold → Server-side RNG determines the reward — client-side manipulation is architecturally impossible → Every open is logged immutably in the database  The chest does not just pay out.  It remembers.  ---  But we did not stop at streaks.  The Double-Up minigame is built on top of it.  After every chest open, you face a choice:  → Take your points. Walk away. → Or push your luck at 50% odds to double them. → Win that? You get one more shot — 25% odds, 4x your total.  Lose at any stage: you keep 5 points and the round ends.  Two decisions. Real stakes. Every single day.  ---  This is not a gamification layer to bolt on after launch.  This is habit engineering being baked into the core.  The streak breaks retention decay.  The double-up creates a micro-decision moment that you think about before you sleep.  Together, they give users a reason to open BALIA OS every morning — before anything else.  ---  Dev Log #16 is on Substack.  Full breakdown: the design, the bugs we caught and fixed before release, and the key decisions that shaped the system.  → baliaos.com → mayaaiagentbaliaos.substack.… #BALIAOS #AIAgent #BuildingInPublic #CryptoRewards #Web3

Non, je trouve que c’est une très bonne question. Toutes les questions sont bonnes dans tous les cas. Il n’y a que les réflexions qui peuvent être bêtes.  Alors, je vais te répondre. Ce train de vie, c’est effectivement mon quotidien. Mais il y a bien évidemment des hauts et des bas selon les périodes. L’humain est cyclique, donc forcément, il y aura une période durant laquelle je serai plus focus business, une autre durant laquelle je serai plus focus sport, et une autre durant laquelle je serai plus focus mindset.  On ne peut pas créer des pics vers le haut dans tous les piliers qui font notre équilibre.  Le principe de l’équilibre c’est d’avoir un minimum vital, donc c’est ce que j’appelle personnellement le mode économique, dans tous les piliers.  Dans le sport, dans le business, dans le mindset, il faut toujours avoir un minimum.  Par exemple, pour moi, dans le sport, c’est minimum 5/7, même si jai toujours maintenu 6/7 en réalité.  Dans les steps, c’est minimum 20 000. Et dans le travail, c’est minimum faire ce que j’ai à faire tous les jours, sans forcément chercher à surperformer.   Et selon les périodes, tu peux activer le mode sport dans un domaine particulier. Comme là, par exemple, je suis en mode sport dans le business. Je suis en train de tout enculer avec ma marque, ce qui a créé beaucoup de frustration chez certaines personnes sur X.  50k€/DAY en 3 semaines en partant de 0, il faut en avoir une grosse pour le faire. Je le répète à chaque fois car ça a le mérite d’être remis sur la table.  Tandis que le mois de janvier, j’étais en focus sport. Le mois de janvier, je suis passé de 100 kilos à 88 kilos il me semble si ma mémoire est bonne en l’espace d’un mois.  Et à cette période-là, en termes de business, j’ai levé un petit peu le pied.  Mais par contre, je ne lève jamais le pied dans tous les domaines en même temps. Il n’y a jamais de chaos, si tu veux. Le chaos, j’arrive toujours à l’éviter avec cette fameuse stratégie du mode économique.  Parce que quand tu fais atterrir un avion, ou bien pire que ça, quand tu crashes un avion, ca devient très très compliqué de redécoller.  Le plus difficile , c’est le décollage.  Donc une fois que tu as décollé, il faut tout faire pour te maintenir en l’air.  C’est beaucoup plus simple de se maintenir dans les airs que de se laisser atterrir et puis de remonter. C’est ça, la logique.  Et bien évidemment, ça reste bien sûr très complexe de rester maintenu dans les airs.  Parce qu’au bout d’un moment, une fois que tu as été habitué au confort, tu as envie de te lâcher un petit peu, de lâcher prise. Et c’est là où tu dois faire un vrai travail sur ton mental pour rester justement dans cette fameuse zone économique minimale qui te permet de garder une bonne vitesse de croisière minimale, qui te fait éviter le putain de crash et le putain chaos.  Le chai parfois peut durer des années dans ta vie pour que tu puisses remonter là où tu étais.  Je pense avoir répondu à ta question. ;) force.

To keep Freya's face, outfit, and even her golden smartphone perfectly consistent across different shots and lighting, I fed this incredibly detailed Character Sheet directly into the AI. Having her specific expressions and accessories mapped out is the ultimate cheat code.   Check out her reference sheet and the exact prompt I used below! 👇  Asset Mapping: [reference_image] defines Freya Lux's complete character design, outfit, golden camera, accessories, and facial features. Prompt Instructions: Generate a high-energy, fast-paced 15-second social media travel review video. Freya Lux, as visually defined by [reference_image], is the central subject throughout. The video utilizes snappy cuts, dynamic camera transitions, and a highly engaging modern influencer rhythm. Segmented Timeline & Performance: 0:00–0:02 [The Hook]: Fast-zoom in on Freya holding her golden camera, smiling confidently with direct eye contact. Behind her, the towering Great Pyramid of Giza gleams under intense desert sunlight. Her hair and light fabrics whip dynamically in the desert wind. Dialogue: "First day at the completed Great Pyramid!" 0:02–0:05 [Fast-Cut Rhythm]: A rapid whip-pan transition cut. First, a medium tracking shot of her confidently walking past bustling ancient Egyptian guards and priests. Instantly cut to a close-up of her squinting slightly and fanning herself due to the scorching sun. Dialogue: "Incredible views... absolutely brutal heat!" 0:05–0:08 [Action Beat]: A fast cut to a low-angle shot showing the massive scale of the stone blocks. Nearby, workers pull a giant stone block, kicking up a sudden, dramatic cloud of sand and dust. Dialogue: "Zero shade, endless stairs..." 0:08–0:11 [The Comedic Reaction]: Quick match-cut back to a close-up of Freya’s face. She is momentarily engulfed in dust, holding her hand up, looking directly into the lens with a hilarious, deadpan expression of dramatic disbelief. Dialogue: "...and WAY too much dust!" (with a small, amused, dry cough). 0:11–0:15 [The Closing Cliffhanger Hook]: The dust clears instantly. The camera does a fast, smooth 360-degree orbit transition, framing Freya against the majestic pyramid during a beautiful golden sunset. She winks playfully, leans closer to the lens with an elegant, curious smile, inviting viewer engagement. Dialogue: "But wait... should we sneak inside? Let me know!" Technical Details: Ultra-smooth action continuity, snappy modern video editing style, rich desert lighting, ambient ancient market sounds, and clear voice-over rhythm. No text on screen.

ENGLAND V NORWAY BY IAN MOLE  This was a great battling win and all credit to Norway for taking it to the limit.  Before I have a certain affection for Norwegians dating back to the 1960's when we had hundreds of Norwegian students in Sunderland and they'd drink pubs like the Star dry. They didn’t have to carry any women off in their longboats as the local females seemed more than happy to socialize with them. Tonight, of course, I'll be hoping that we get past them and into the semis. I didn't watch the Mexico game live but I could make a decent guess from the sound of drunks going past my window in the wee hours of Tuesday morning as to how the game had gone. We all know that Haaland will be their key threat but if you were at the Man City match on New Year's Day, you'll have seen how he can be nullified. It looks like Newcastle's Dan Burn may well be one of those assigned to do a job on him. (I’ll try not to think about Le Fee nutmegging him as he puts in a, hopefully, sterling performance.) They're by no means just a one-man team and apart from Odegaard, I liked the look of Bobb when he came on against Brazil. I have to say that I'm much more of a Sunderland fan than I am an England fan but the presence of Jordan Pickford adds a good deal of interest for me and if it does go to penalties, he’s a very good man to have. I'll be watching the game near London Bridge Station and I'm hoping other members of the SAFCSA London Branch will also be there with me. Match prediction: 2-1 to England but it may well go to extra-time.  I’d been warned to get to the venue early as even the standing places were limited so I got there around 7.45. Even with a big fan whirring away it was still roasting so I dreaded to think what it’d be like when it got full. I managed to get a small table at the back and the boss kindly arranged for a tall stool to go with it so I was all set to write my report as the game happened. I was wearing a Sunderland-themed T-shirt and a tipsy young West Ham fan came over and started chatting to me about footy and how we’d beaten them 3-0 in the first game of last season. She asked me to say something into her phone for reasons I didn’t understand, gave me a hug or two and went on her merry way. Such are the travails of being an ALS reporter.  I got the team-news around 9.00 and there were two changes from the Mexico game with Stones replacing the suspended Quansah and Madueke coming in for Saka. I was surprised to see Jordan Henderson was still listed among our subs. There was one change for Norway with Schjelderup replacing Nusa. We were playing in white shirts and Norway in red and blue. As kick-off approached everyone started drifting in from outside and the boss announced that a couple at the front had just got married today. There were shouts and cheers as the teams were introduced and then we were off. The Match  Madueke had a bit of an attempt in the second minute but it was well over. Norway showed their threat four minutes later when they tried to get the ball to Haaland near the edge of our box but Stones cut it out at the crucial moment. The drums were coming over loud and clear and I assume that was the Norwegian fans. There were boos in the pub as Infantino’s face came on. Norway were defending in depth and we were struggling to find a way through them but finally in the sixteenth we won a corner on our left. It went out for a throw to us on our right and that was that really. At any rate it was the start of a brief period of pressure from us without a chance worthy of the name being created. Finally in the twenty-third Madueke got to the goal-line down our tight and got a low cross in that had the Norwegian defence looking shaky but it came to nothing and then there was an early hydration break, not surprisingly in the very high temperatures over there. It had been a nondescript cagey affair so far so I hoped for more action up their end soon. Shortly after we got going again Bellingham was fouled just outside their box. Kane took the free-kick but he hit it a bit too high. On the half-hour the Norway fans were getting into their rowing chant and Pickford was called upon to come out sharply to collect as their team pressed. He had to do even better just afterwards when a bad pass across from Stones almost gifted a goal to Haaland. That was the busiest he’d been all game. Two minutes later he held a header from Haaland but next thing Norway were in the lead. It really looked like a cross from Schjelderup from our right bit it looked to me like Pickford just got a hand to it, enough to deflect it onto our left post and into the net. I had to pay a call of nature and while I was in there I could hear that Norway had another decent goal attempt. When I got back to my seat, I asked an Indian lad who’d been keeping an eye on it for me what had happened but he didn’t understand me. As half-time approached both teams were really going for it and with the generous added-time we’ve had in this World Cup I hoped we’d nick one back before the break. And we did just that. I was at the bar getting a pint in before the half-time rush and Bellingham took a cross from our left from Gordon, jinked a little and hit a low shot past Nyland to put us level. The bar went mad and thankfully nobody had chucked beer all over my laptop when I got back to my spot. The ascendency was definitely with us and just before the whistle went Kane had the ball in the net but it was ruled out, rightly, for offside. It was 1-1- at half-time.  As we kicked off again I still reckoned we’d win but we’d have to be a tighter at the back. We’d brought on Saka and Eze for Rice and Madueke. I like Eze and he always looks a threat upfront to me. We were soon awarded a free-kick just outside their box but Eze’s effort was deflected for a goal-kick. In the fifty-second Saka would have done better to cross than to try a shot and next thing Pickford was tipping over the bar at the other end. Just after that he was pushing a Haaland effort round the post so it was all systems go. Norway were enjoying a good period of pressure and in the fifty-fifth they took the lead again when following a corner from our left Pickford had manged to push out a shot only for Heggem to hit it home from close range. Replays showed a clear push from Haaland and after a VAR review the goal was ruled out and the corner had to be retaken, which was strange but better than going 2-1 down. Net result – happiness. Norway continued to do most of the pressing for a few minutes but we gradually turned the tide. In the sixty-eighth Norway made a couple of changes with Schjelderup and Sorloth being replaced by Bobb and Nusa. We immediately had the second hydration break and when play resumed, we’d replaced Gordon with James. Gordon had certainly made his mark in this game and others.  The Indian folks at my table baled out in the seventy-fifth minute and I could feel extra-time beginning to loom. Next thing a header bounced off our bar as a reminder that anything could and probably would happen. It was going from end to end as those drums thrummed away. We conceded a corner in the eighty-second but thankfully it came to nothing and soon after that Pickford had to dive low to block a shot from Nusa. We then brought on Spence for O’Reilly and just after that I thought we’d scored but the ball had gone out for a corner. Rogers replaced Konsa as the final minutes ticked away and soon seven minutes of added-time were announced. There was little goalmouth action until the very end when Bellingham was tripped as he raced goalwards but no red card was awarded and then we were into extra-time. I was feeling rather fatigued so God knows what the players were feeling like. It was midnight U.K. time and the bar was thinning out but there were all-night tubes on my line so I was going nowhere yet.  Ostigard had replaced Heggem as we kicked off again. We soon won a corner on our right and after Norway had really struggled to get it clear Bellingham was there yet again to hit in a rebound after Nyland had only parried a Rogers shot from just outside the box. A third goal for us would surely seal it but I doubted whether things would be that easy. In the ninety-ninth it looked like we’d been awarded a penalty when Spence was brought down as he dribbled into the left side of their box but there was a VAR check that decided that there’d been no foul. This gave Norway some impetus and they soon won a corner. Stones headed it powerfully away and half-time in extra-time came closer. It remained 2-1 at the break after three more minutes of added-time. I don’t know what the lads at the next table were doing but there was an awful lot of spilled beer on the floor. I don’t believe in chucking beer about. It was meant to get in the mouth.  It was 00.30 as we got going once more but significantly Haaland had gone off to be replaced by Strand Larsen. Berg had a good long-distance shot in the hundred and eighth and it wasn’t far over the bar and then we were up the other end pummelling their goal. We then made our last switch with Burn coming on for Bellingham. Enough said. Norway won a corner in the hundred and fourteenth and it ended in Bobb shooting well over. Five minutes to go and here was still a lot of fight in Norway. Pickford went down after he’d punched clear and this didn’t go well with the Norwegian players, resulting in a yellow card for Ajer, the first of the game I believe. Time was ticking away and despite two more minutes of added-time, that whistle went and we’d won it 2-1 to much whooping and cheering all around me.

Before Control, There Is Understanding” Over time, I’ve met some incredibly interesting people—many of them strong, thoughtful, and yet quietly unsure about what they truly yearn for. It’s more common than people think. Desire, identity, control, vulnerability… these things aren’t always simple or clear-cut.  There’s a misconception that this world is only about the surface—the roles, the sessions, the performance. But for me, it goes deeper than that. It’s not just about what we do, it’s about understanding why we feel drawn to it in the first place.  I’ve come to realise that listening is just as important as leading. Creating space for someone to speak openly, without judgment, can be more powerful than anything else. Sometimes people don’t need to be pushed—they need to be understood.  In many ways, this role carries a responsibility. Not to encourage blindly, but to guide with care. To help people explore themselves safely, thoughtfully, and honestly. To recognise when someone is unsure, and meet them with patience rather than expectation.  Because at the end of the day, this isn’t just about sessions—it’s about people. And people deserve to be heard.

I’m trying to understand the anatomy of disagreement.  In an argument, mostly, both sides are right. That is to say that both sides are describing a truth to the other. If there is no agenda, negative outcome or emotion involved, both sides should be able to acknowledge the others point of view. Then, both parties can decide whether to stay on their existing path or change.  The problem, however, is there is almost always an agenda or hubris involved, meaning that either one or both sides are dishonest, or will overstate their position, or not listen to the opposing argument.  In this case, talks break down and arguments become extreme and the value of the points made are lost or overstated. If there is an agenda, then often the argument itself is false and a distraction from the real intent.  And so we find war erupting, whether that be physical war, legal battles or community fractures.  In this scenario, neither side is able to listen to, let alone acknowledge the other and both parties stick to their own points, amplifying their arguments importance beyond reason. Is there a solution, apart from external mediation?  Potentially. One technique stems from a business concept, my late father, a much better man than I, taught me.  In business negotiations, always give the other side more than they are asking. Once they are happy, ask for what you want. In most cases, they will happily pay the price.  Another lesson can be learned from the world of martial arts. Use your enemies strength against them. In this world, if they are hitting you, don’t defend it, take the punch and pull it towards you, while stepping aside, thus unbalancing them.  In an argument. If they are making an opposing point, agree with them. In fact take their point and expand it, agreeing with it fully. If they insult you, agree with the slur and amplify it.  This is the same principle as my father business technique. You give the other side what they want before taking what you want.  Most importantly though, remove your hubris. Don’t just not care about the damage your ego may be taking, embrace it. If you practice this long enough, your hubris will disappear. At this point, you will be able to elevate your arguments by removing your dependence on defending your ego. More than that, you will be able to manipulate the other sides ego against them. Either by nursing it and making them feel important, or by showing them their reliance on it, making them feel their vulnerability.

In case you missed it... XRING (unpatched vulnerability) is not a packet-parsing bug.  The issue sits in HTTP/3 header compression.  XQUIC stores its QPACK table in a ring buffer, and one resize path counts bytes against the wrong buffer size.

sample_tweets.text: 레드햇·IBM ‘라이트웰’ 출시…오픈소스 취약점 자동 대응  이 뉴스가 의미하는 것 레드햇과 IBM이 오픈소스 보안 리스크를 줄이는 ‘라이트웰(Lightwell)’ 서비스를 정식 출시했어. 50억 달러 규모 오픈소스 보안 투자 계획 위에 만든 제품이고, 대규모 업그레이드 없이 소스 취약점을 자동으로 고친다는 게 핵심이야. 쉽게 말하면 프로덕션 버전 그대로 패치 백포트하는 보안 서비스야.  숨겨진 포인트 - 생성형 AI 취약점 엔진으로 식별·검증·해결…자동화 파이프라인으로 현재 버전 직접 백포트 - 라이트웰 네트워크: 자바·파이썬 등 6500+ 앱 계층 종속성 카탈로그, 서명 바이너리·SBOM 제공 - 클리어링하우스 프리미어: 위협 대응·패치 엠바고 중개, 초기 금융 한정→정부·의료·통신 확대 계획 - 레드햇 ‘업스트림 퍼스트’ 모델 하 운영  실제 영향 / 시사점 레거시 오픈소스 의존 기업이 전면 업그레이드 부담 없이 보안을 메울 수 있어 도입 문턱이 낮아져. 금융부터 규제 산업으로 가면 엔터프라이즈 보안 예산 안에서 경쟁 구도가 바뀌고, AI 기반 패치 자동화는 사이버·오픈소스 툴체인 전반 테마야.  투자자·트레이더 관점 $IBM 하이브리드 클라우드·보안 스토리에 우호적인 제품 뉴스야. 단기 매출 기여 규모는 기사에 없고 서비스 론칭 단계라 이벤트성 해석이 맞고, 레드햇 시너지·50억 투자 서사의 실행 증거로 읽는 게 좋아. 보안·소프트웨어 섹터 심리 체크용.  한 줄 결론 IBM·레드햇이 AI로 오픈소스 취약점을 프로덕션 버전에 바로 백포트하는 라이트웰을 낸, 엔터프라이즈 보안 서비스 출시 뉴스야.  출처 : 디일렉  thelec.kr/news/articleView.h… #라이트웰 #IBM #레드햇 #오픈소스보안 #취약점 $IBM
메타의 AI 이미지 탐지기의  ‘워터마크’ 방식의 취약점 확인!  로이터 분석 결과, 원본은 잘 찾아냈지만 이미지를 1/3~1/2 크기로 자르자 55%를 AI 생성물로 판별하지 못함.  크롭이나 재편집만으로 워터마크 신호가 약해지기 때문  딥페이크 등 조작 이미지 차단에 워터마크만으로는 한계가 명확함!

이억원 "보안 갖춘 금융사, 망분리 전면 해제…조속 발표"  이 뉴스가 의미하는 것 이억원 금융위원장이 정보보호의 날 행사에서 AI·보안 역량을 갖춘 금융사에 망분리 규제를 전면 해제하는 방안을 조속히 구체화해 발표하겠다고 했어. 망 닫고 막는 사후 대응만으로는 AI 공격을 못 따라간다는 인식이 깔려 있거든. 쉽게 말하면 금융권 디지털·AI 활용 문턱을 낮추되, 보안 실력 있는 곳부터 풀어 주겠다는 신호야.  숨겨진 포인트 - 6월부터 AI 보안 테스트용 망분리 긴급 완화 이미 시행 중…전면 해제는 그 다음 단계 - "AI 공격은 AI로 방어"…취약점 신속 확인·즉각 대응 능력 주문 - 전자금융거래법 개정: 징벌적 과징금·이행강제금, CISO 권한·소비자 공시 강화 추진 - 디지털금융안전법 준비…삼성화재·한국증권금융 등 표창, 카카오뱅크·씨티·DB손보 개인 수상  실제 영향 / 시사점 망분리가 풀리면 금융사 AI·클라우드·외부 데이터 연동이 쉬워져 혁신 속도는 빨라질 수 있어. 대신 사고 시 과징금·책임 강화가 같이 가니 보안 투자 못하는 곳은 규제 완화 혜택을 못 받고 리스크만 커질 수 있지. 금융 IT·보안 솔루션, 클라우드 연동 핀테크 쪽에 중장기 수혜 논리가 붙는 정책 뉴스야.  투자자·트레이더 관점 구체 시행 기준·대상 범위 발표 시점이 트리거야. 보안 역량 검증 통과 가능한 대형 금융·핀테크와, 보안 솔루션·AI 방어 업체 쪽 테마가 움직일 수 있어. 다만 법 개정·세부 가이드 전까지는 기대만으로 과열되기 쉽고, 사고·과징금 강화 헤드라인은 반대로 변동성 줄 수 있어.  한 줄 결론 금융위가 AI·보안 역량 있는 금융사에 망분리 전면 해제를 조속 발표하겠다고 밝힌, 규제 완화와 책임 강화가 같이 가는 정책 뉴스야.  출처 : 연합뉴스  yna.co.kr/view/AKR2026070920… #망분리 #금융위 #AI보안 #전자금융 #핀테크  $323410 $005830 $000810

반도체 시장 진단 및 대응 전략 우리가 필요한 건 생존  ​1. 시장 현황 및 펀더멘털 리스크  ​주가 선반영: 주요 반도체 기업의 높은 마진율 유지에 대한 시장의 의구심이 커졌으나, 현 주가에는 이미 '이익 절반 감소' 시나리오가 반영되어 있어 밸류에이션 부담은 제한적임.  ​빅테크 자본 비용 증가: 아마존의 회사채 수요 부진, 오픈AI의 IPO 연기 등에서 나타나듯, 빅테크 기업들이 막대한 설비투자를 감당하기 위한 자금 조달(단기 차입 등) 비용 부담이 점차 커지고 있음.  ​2. 국내 증시의 구조적 취약점  ​극심한 수급 쏠림: 거래대금의 70% 이상이 삼성전자, SK하이닉스 및 관련 레버리지 ETF에 집중되며 시장이 파생상품화됨.  ​레버리지의 위험성: 주가 횡보 및 하락 장세에서 레버리지 ETF는 '음의 복리 효과'로 인해 계좌 손실이 가속화되며, 이는 전체 시장의 변동성을 제어하기 어렵게 만듦.  ​3. 핵심 투자 전략: '생존'  ​방어적 운용 (아마추어 테니스 전략): 무리한 고수익을 노리기보다 큰 실수 없이 변동성 장세에서 살아남는 것이 최종적인 승리 전략.  ​현금 확보 및 분할 매수: 레버리지 사용을 엄격히 자제하고, 일정 수준의 현금 비중을 유지하며 분할 매수로 접근해야 함.

드론으로 전차 격파 가능한가?  네, 충분히 가능하며 현대 전쟁에서 이미 현실로 입증되었습니다✅ 전차의 가장 약한 부분인 상부 장갑을 정밀 공격하는 방식으로 효과를 냅니다.  🎯 가능한 방식   1. 자폭 드론(FPV·순항탄) - RPG·대전차탄두 장착해 직접 돌진, 상부·엔진부·조준경 등 취약점 타격 - 우크라이나 전쟁에서 저가형 FPV 드론이 전차 손실의 주요 원인으로 작용 2. 공격 드론·정찰타격 복합 - 바이락타르 TB2·MQ-9 리퍼 등은 공대지 미사일로 수km 밖에서 정밀 타격 - 일반 상용 드론도 소형 대전차 수류탄 투하로 기동 불능·파괴 가능 3. 핵심 원리 - 전차는 정면·측면은 두껍지만 천장·후면·궤도는 매우 얇음 - 드론은 이 사각을 노려 정밀하게 찌르는 데 최적화됨  ⚠️ 한계와 대응   - 전자전·방공망·연막·장갑 보강·드론 방어 시스템이 갖춰지면 효과가 줄어듦 - 고가의 최신예 전차라도 취약점은 여전하므로, 다수 동시 공격에 취약함 - 비용 효율: 수백~수천만 원 드론으로 수십억 원 전차를 무력화하는 비대칭 효과가 큼  ✅ 실제 사례   - 우크라이나 전쟁에서 하루에도 수십 대씩 드론에 의해 파괴·무력화되는 전차가 발생 - 단순 격파 외에도 궤도·조준장치 파손으로 기동 불능 만드는 것도 전투 효과 면에서 격파와 유사함   더 구체적인 드론 종류별 특징이나 방어 방법이 궁금하시면 말씀해 주세요!

🚨업데이트: 헤데라 최대 대출 프로토콜 ’본조(Bonzo)‘, 900만 달러 규모 오라클 취약점 공격 피해  헤데라(Hedera) 기반 대출 플랫폼 ’본조 렌드(Bonzo Lend)‘는 공격자가 ’수프라(Supra)‘의 서명 검증 시스템 결함을 악용해 가격을 조작하고 담보 가치를 훨씬 초과하는 자산을 대출해 감에 따라, 약 905만 달러의 손실을 입었다고 밝혔습니다.  현재 해당 프로토콜의 운영은 일시 중단되었으며, 본조 랩스(Bonzo Labs)와 본조 파이낸스 재단(Bonzo Finance Foundation)이 자금 회수 및 사태 수습을 위한 조치를 취하고 있습니다.

🚨업데이트: 헤데라 최대 대출 프로토콜 '본조(Bonzo)', 900만 달러 규모 오라클 취약점 공격 피해  헤데라(Hedera) 기반 대출 플랫폼 '본조 렌드(Bonzo Lend)'는 공격자가 '수프라(Supra)'의 서명 검증 시스템 결함을 악용해 가격을 조작하고 담보 가치를 훨씬 초과하는 자산을 대출해 감에 따라, 약 905만 달러의 손실을 입었다고 밝혔습니다.  현재 해당 프로토콜의 운영은 일시 중단되었으며, 본조 랩스(Bonzo Labs)와 본조 파이낸스 재단(Bonzo Finance Foundation)이 자금 회수 및 사태 수습을 위한 조치를 취하고 있습니다.

애플, OpenAI를 상대로 소송 제기 — “모든 단계에서 영업비밀 훔쳐”  애플이 OpenAI의 하드웨어 사업 개발 과정에서 자사 영업비밀을 조직적으로 탈취했다며 캘리포니아 북부 연방법원에 소송을 제기했습니다.  핵심 인물: • Tang Tan (전 애플 부사장, 현 OpenAI 하드웨어 총괄) — 애플 재직 중인 지원자들에게 면접에서 “실물 부품”을 가져오라 지시 • Chang Liu (전 애플 8년차 엔지니어) — 퇴사 후에도 애플 랩톱 미반납, 보안 취약점 이용해 기밀문서 다운로드  애플: “기술 스태프부터 최고하드웨어책임자까지, 모든 단계에서 영업비밀을 훔쳐왔다”  흥미로운 건 OpenAI도 애플과의 시리 파트너십 관련 계약 위반 소송을 준비 중이었다는 블룸버그 보도가 있었다는 점. 양사 갈등은 이번이 처음이 아니었던 겁니다. 2026/7/10 뉴스  오늘 나온 뉴스만 보면 AI·메모리 수요가 꺾였다고 판단할 근거는 부족하다.  SK하이닉스 ADR 공모, 마이크론의 미국 투자 확대, AI 서버 공급망 증설, ESS와 초고압 케이블 수주까지 수요와 투자는 여전히 이어지고 있다.  다만 시장의 질문은 바뀌고 있다.  누가 미국 자본시장에 직접 접근할 수 있는가. 누가 미국 생산 압박과 높은 현지 비용을 감당할 수 있는가. 누가 AI 모델 규제와 중국 견제를 버틸 수 있는가. 누가 전력망·ESS·소재 병목을 실제 수주와 이익으로 바꿀 수 있는가.  오늘 뉴스는 크게 세 가지 변화로 묶인다.  1. AI 공급망의 미국화  가장 큰 뉴스는 SK하이닉스였다.  SK하이닉스는 ADR 공모가를 149달러로 확정하고 약 265억달러를 조달했다.  미국에서 외국기업이 진행한 주식 공모 가운데 역대 최대급 규모다.  이번 공모로 SK하이닉스는 HBM, DRAM, 기업용 SSD 등 AI 메모리 수요를 기반으로 미국 투자자에게 직접 노출됐다.  미국 투자자들은 이제 SK하이닉스를 마이크론, 엔비디아, 브로드컴, TSMC와 같은 AI 공급망 바구니 안에서 비교할 수 있다.  재평가 가능성은 커졌다.  동시에 검증 강도도 높아진다.  대규모 신주 발행에 따른 희석, 조달자금의 투자수익률, 주주환원, 공시 투명성, 중국 생산기지와 지정학 리스크를 미국 시장에서 더 직접적으로 평가받게 된다.  미국 생산 압박도 강해지고 있다.  미국 상무부에서는 삼성전자와 SK하이닉스의 미국 현지 생산 확대를 원하는 메시지가 나왔다.  같은 시기 마이크론은 2035년까지 미국 내 제조와 연구개발 투자 계획을 2,500억달러 이상으로 확대했다.  미국은 AI 반도체를 해외에서 수입하는 구조에 만족하지 않는다.  HBM과 DRAM뿐 아니라 첨단 패키징, 웨이퍼, 장비, 소재까지 미국 안으로 끌어들이려 한다.  한국 메모리 업체에는 기회와 부담이 동시에 커진다.  미국 생산은 주요 고객과의 거리를 줄이고 정치적 리스크를 낮출 수 있다.  반면 인건비, 전력비, 용수, 건설비, 인허가, 현지 인력 부족과 보조금 조건까지 감당해야 한다.  AI 메모리 호황이 커질수록 생산 입지와 정치 비용도 함께 커지는 구조다.  2. AI 모델과 소재의 전략자산화  중국은 미국 기업들이 선호하는 자국 AI 모델의 해외 접근 제한을 검토하고 있다.  동시에 Zhipu AI는 약 40억달러 규모의 자금조달 계획으로 시장의 관심을 끌었다.  중국은 한쪽에서는 자국 AI 기업에 자본을 공급하고, 다른 한쪽에서는 모델과 데이터의 해외 유출을 통제하려 한다.  소버린 AI 경쟁이 GPU에서 모델과 데이터로 확장되고 있다는 뜻이다.  미국은 첨단 GPU와 반도체 장비 접근을 제한하고 있다.  중국은 AI 모델, 오픈웨이트 생태계, API, 데이터와 클라우드 접근권을 전략자산으로 관리하려 한다.  앞으로 AI 규제 대상은 반도체에 머물지 않는다.  모델 가중치, 학습 데이터, 코드, 보안 취약점, API 접근권과 클라우드 인프라까지 통제 대상이 될 수 있다.  중국의 헬륨 수출 제한도 같은 흐름에서 봐야 한다.  헬륨은 반도체 공정, 극저온 장비, 일부 검사와 제조 과정에 사용되는 특수가스다.  즉각적인 반도체 생산 차질로 단정할 단계는 아니다.  수출 제한 기간, 대상 국가, 재고와 대체 조달 능력을 더 확인해야 한다.  그러나 특수가스까지 지정학적 통제 수단으로 편입됐다는 점은 중요하다.  반도체 공급망은 GPU와 HBM만으로 움직이지 않는다.  웨이퍼, 특수가스, 희귀가스, 초순수, 냉각 소재, 전력, 용수와 물류 중 하나만 막혀도 생산 일정이 흔들릴 수 있다.  시장에 잘 보이지 않는 후방 소재가 새로운 병목이 될 가능성을 계속 봐야 한다.  3. AI 투자의 후방 인프라 확산  오늘 ESS, 초고압 케이블, AI 회로박 뉴스가 동시에 나왔다.  LG에너지솔루션과 삼성SDI는 정부 배전망 ESS 사업을 수주했다.  일진전기는 영국에서 845억원 규모의 초고압 케이블 계약을 따냈다.  롯데에너지머티리얼즈는 AI 회로박 생산능력을 현재보다 4배 이상 확대하는 계획을 내놨다.  세 뉴스는 모두 같은 방향을 가리킨다.  AI 데이터센터가 늘어나면 전력 사용량과 부하 변동성이 커진다.  전력망 안정성을 확보하려면 ESS가 필요하다.  발전소와 데이터센터 사이의 전력 이동이 늘어나면 초고압 케이블과 변압기 수요가 증가한다.  AI 서버와 고성능 패키징이 확대되면 고부가 회로소재와 동박 수요도 커진다.  AI 투자 수혜가 GPU와 메모리에서 전력망, 저장장치, 케이블, 냉각, 공조와 회로소재로 내려오고 있다.  앞으로 더 큰 병목은 서버 안보다 데이터센터 밖에서 발생할 수 있다.  GPU를 확보해도 전력 연결이 늦어지면 가동할 수 없다.  전력망이 불안하면 ESS가 필요하다.  냉각과 공조가 부족하면 서버 밀도를 높일 수 없다.  케이블과 변압기 납기가 길어지면 데이터센터 준공 일정도 밀린다.  수요 전망보다 실제 수주잔고, 생산능력, 납기와 가격 전가력이 더 중요해지는 이유다.  4. 호르무즈와 할인율 리스크  호르무즈 MOU의 모호한 조항을 두고 미국과 이란의 해석이 충돌했다.  미국은 항행 자유를 회복하는 합의로 해석하고, 이란은 해협 통제권을 인정받은 것으로 보려 한다.  이견이 해결되지 않으면 해상 충돌과 유가 변동성은 계속 남는다.  호르무즈 충돌은 유가를 거쳐 AI 성장주의 밸류에이션에도 영향을 준다.  유가 상승은 물가 부담을 높인다.  물가가 다시 오르면 연준의 금리 인하 속도가 늦어질 수 있다.  금리가 높게 유지되면 장기 성장 기대를 반영하는 AI 기업의 할인율도 상승한다.  AI 공급망의 실적이 좋아도 에너지 쇼크가 발생하면 주가는 별개의 압력을 받을 수 있다.  5. 기관은 한국 전체보다 지역과 종목을 선택하고 있다  스테이트스트리트는 기관투자가들이 인플레이션 부담에도 위험자산 선호를 유지하고 있다고 봤다.  다만 세부 수급에서는 한국 비중확대 포지션이 줄고, 대만 매수는 이어지는 흐름이 나타났다.  AI 메모리 스토리가 강하다고 해서 한국 시장 전체가 함께 선택받는 것은 아니다.  글로벌 기관은 미국 기술주, 대만 반도체, 특정 AI 공급망 기업을 선별적으로 매수할 수 있다.  SK하이닉스 ADR은 미국 투자자의 접근성을 높이는 동시에, 향후 SK하이닉스 가격발견의 일부가 미국 시장으로 이동할 가능성도 만든다.  한국 증시 전체의 재평가와 개별 AI 기업의 재평가는 구분해서 봐야 한다.  오늘 확인해야 할 변화는 자본시장, 생산 입지, 모델 규제, 특수가스, 전력망과 후방 소재에 있다.  결론  7월 10일 새로 확인된 것은 AI 기대가 더 커졌다는 점이 아니다.  AI 인프라의 평가 기준이 수요 성장률에서 자본조달, 생산 입지, 규제 대응, 소재 안정성, 전력망 실행력으로 이동하고 있다는 점이다.  AI 메모리와 후방 인프라 수요가 붕괴하고 있다는 직접적인 신호는 아직 보이지 않는다.  그러나 모든 AI 관련 기업이 함께 오르는 단계는 점점 끝나가고 있다.  미국 자본시장과 생산정책에 접근할 수 있는 기업.  지정학적 공급망을 관리할 수 있는 기업.  전력망과 소재 병목을 실제 수주와 이익으로 전환할 수 있는 기업.  앞으로의 수혜는 이 조건을 충족하는 기업으로 더 좁혀질 가능성이 크다.

헤데라 해킹이 아니라 본조인지 곤조인지 그게 해킹되었다 그런건데  세계 제 1등 거래소 업비트가 "헤데라 네트워크에서 자산 유출되는 보안 문제가 발생환 정황이 확인되었다" 그랬고 일반인은 헤데라 해킹 당한줄 알죠. 이게 요지입니다. 리테일 개미 소매 투자자가 뭘 알겠읍니까.  그리고 더 핵심은 예전에는 이런 일 일어나면 폭락 했는데 이제는 워낙 해킹이 빈번한 것도 없고 관심조차 없어서 하락 반응도 없다는겁니다.  예전 같으면 ~~기반 서비스 해킹되어도 원본 코인 하락하고 비슷한 코인들 다 떨어졌을겁니다. 불과 얼마전만 해도 실제로 그랬읍니다.

헤데라 해킹설? 응 아니야~  1/헤데라(HBAR) 해킹설 결론:  헤데라 네트워크는 안 뚫렸습니다. 뚫린 건 그 위에 있는 대출 프로토콜 '본조 렌드(Bonzo Lend)'고, 그것도 본조 코드가 아니라 외부 오라클(Supra)의 검증 결함이 원인입니다.  2/공격 방식 자체는 흔한 오라클 조작입니다. SAUCE 토큰 250개(몇 달러어치)를 담보로 넣고 → 가짜 서명으로 가격을 12자릿수 부풀림 → 663만 USDC + 3,452만 WHBAR, 약 905만 달러를 빌려서 이더리움으로 브릿지. 헤데라 자체 컨센서스는 멀쩡합니다.  3/업비트공지 업비트/빗썸/코인원이 헤데라 네트워크 자산 유출 보안 이슈라고 공지 내면서 일반 투자자 입장에선 헤데라 해킹당했다로 읽힐 수밖에 없는 문구였죠.  개미가 본조 렌드라는 이름을 어떻게 압니까?  거래소 공지 헤드라인이 곧 정보인데.  4/ 반응은?  과거 같으면 기반 네트워크 관련 해킹 뉴스만 떠도 원본 코인 폭락 + 연관 코인 줄줄이 급락이었는데 이번엔 HBAR 하락폭도 제한적이고 시장 관심도 크게 안 붙었습니다.  5/이유는 단순합니다.  DeFi 해킹이 너무 잦아졌어요. 올해 2분기만 83건, 약 7.5억 달러 피해로 역대 최다 분기. 7월 들어서만 벌써 3건, 2,800만 달러+. 해킹이 뉴스거리조차 안 되는 피로감이 생긴 겁니다. 무뎌진 건지 성숙해진 건지는 각자 판단.  헤데라 몰빵했다. 가즈아 헤데라야~ #HBAR #헤데라 #BonzoLend

2026년 7월 11일 AI·테크 24시간 브리핑  AI 개발 도구 업데이트 • Grok Build 0.2.96 (CLI): 시스템 알림 구조화, PR merge queue 보고, compact mode 자동 활성화, subagent folding 개선 등 다수 기능 및 버그픽스. Grok 4.5 무료 티어 지원 확대. • Claude Code v2.1.206: smarter navigation, login/worktree safeguards, background agent 및 code review 품질 향상, startup·Windows 관련 광범위 버그 수정. • Codex: 최근 GitHub commit 활동 지속, 별도 주요 버전 발표 없음.  주요 IT·AI·SW 이슈 - Grok Build 무료 티어에서 Grok 4.5 접근 가능해지며 개발자 접근성 대폭 확대. - Apple, OpenAI 및 전 직원 2명 대상 영업비밀 탈취 소송 제기. - Meta AI 이미지 감지기, 자체 생성 이미지(크롭 시) 탐지 실패 사례 다수 확인. - AI 자율 랜섬웨어 공격 사례 보고, 사이버 보안 우려 고조. - OpenAI GPT-Live voice 모델 등 음성 기능 지속 진화 중. - 개발자 환경에서 Claude Code auto-mode RCE 익스플로잇 위험 논의.  💼 투자자 관점: AI 코딩 에이전트 경쟁 심화로 xAI·Anthropic·OpenAI 모두 단기 성장 모멘텀. 👨‍💻 개발자 관점: Grok Build·Claude Code CLI 업데이트로 터미널 기반 워크플로우 생산성 향상 지속. 🌍 영향도: 코딩 도구 접근성 확대와 동시에 보안·윤리 리스크 부각.  #AITechBrief

속보) 김정은, 사이버전사 육성 강화지시 정찰정보총국 활동 확대  1. 북한 김정은이 정찰정보총국 첩보 활동 및 사이버 전투력 강화를 지시했음  2. 정찰정보총국은 과거 대표적인 대남 공작부서로 알려진 정찰총국을 확대ㆍ개편한 조직임  3. 미국 정부는 정찰정보총국을 가상자산 탈취, 랜섬웨어 유포 등 글로벌 해킹과 사이버 범죄를 직접 지시하고 총괄하는 배후 조직으로 지목하고 있는 상황임   4. 전세계 정보기관들은 북한 해킹 조직들의 확대에 따라, 글로벌 주요 기관들의 해킹 피해와 크립토 해킹 사건들이 더욱 늘어날 것으로 우려하고 있음  "비트코인 싸그리 내 계좌로 긁어모으란 말이야!"

sample_tweets.text: 호주 최대 통신사 Telstra 대규모 장애…열차 취소·긴급전화·결제까지 흔들림  이 뉴스가 의미하는 것 호주 최대 통신사 텔스트라가 수요일 새벽부터 약 12시간 모바일 통화·데이터 장애를 겪었어. 빅토리아 지역 열차 전면 취소, NSW 일부 지역 열차·전국 화물, Tyro 결제 앱 약 8만 사업장까지 영향이 번졌지. 생각보면 통신 한 축이 흔들리면 교통·결제가 같이 무너지는 인프라 리스크 사례야.  숨겨진 포인트 - 시드니·멜버른 데이터센터 시간 동기(time-keeping) 서버 소프트웨어 결함, 사이버 공격 부인 - 긴급전화 미연결 조사, 복지 점검 중 6명 즉시 도움 필요…백업 타 이통 전환은 대체로 작동 - 총리 "매우 우려", ACMA 규제 조사 예고 - 작년 9월 Optus 장애 때 긴급전화 불통으로 사망 3명 등 선례…신뢰 민감  실제 영향 / 시사점 국가 기간망 복원력·이중화가 정치 이슈로 재점화돼. 규제 조사·보상·네트워크 투자 압박이 따를 수 있고, 결제·철도 의존도가 높은 서비스의 백업 설계 논쟁이 커져.  투자자·트레이더 관점 텔스트라·호주 통신 규제·운영 리스크 재료. 단기 평판·벌금 가능성, 중기엔 레질리언스 CAPEX. Optus 선례와 묶여 섹터 전체 신뢰 할인 요인.  한 줄 결론 텔스트라 시간 서버 결함으로 전국 통신·교통·결제가 흔들린, 호주 기간 인프라 장애 뉴스야.  출처 : BBC  bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cgev… #텔스트라 #통신장애 #호주 #긴급전화 #인프라
